title: carya pallida var. arenicola
intro: Carya pallida var. arenicola, also known as sand shagbark hickory, is a variety of shagbark hickory tree native to the prairies of the Midwestern United States. The bark of the Carya pallida var. arenicola tree is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant has been used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and wellness, with benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and boosting the immune system. Carya pallida var. arenicola is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be administered orally to reap its health benefits.
